# Hosting Options - Full Documentation

# Anypoint Platform Hosting Overview

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Host the Anypoint Platform control plane and runtime plane in different locations to meet your
security, data privacy, and compliance requirements. Choose from MuleSoft-managed cloud options or
self-hosted environments to control where your data is stored and processed. Each option keeps your
data within the physical location your organization requires.

## Control Plane and Runtime Plane

Anypoint Platform architecture separates into two distinct planes that can be hosted independently:

![Diagram of contents of the control plane and runtime plane](_images/intro-control-runtime-planes.png)

Control plane

The components you use to design, deploy, and manage APIs and Mule applications. The control plane
includes Anypoint Design Center, Anypoint Management Center, and Anypoint Exchange.

Runtime plane

The components where your APIs and Mule applications are deployed and made available to your users.
The runtime plane includes the Mule runtime engine and supporting services.

## Control Plane Hosting Options

MuleSoft provides cloud-managed and self-hosted control plane options. This table summarizes each
option, including data residency and applicable compliance standards.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Control Plane</th><th>Platform URL</th><th>Data Residency</th><th>Description</th><th>Compliance / Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>US Cloud (default)</p></td><td><p><code>anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><p>United States</p></td><td><p>MuleSoft-managed control plane hosted in the US. Supports the widest range of runtime regions globally.</p></td><td><p>Standard commercial terms</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>EU Cloud</p></td><td><p><code>eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><p>European Union</p></td><td><p>MuleSoft-managed control plane hosted in the EU. Supports EU-based runtime regions.</p></td><td><p>EU data residency</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Canada Cloud</p></td><td><p><code>ca1.platform.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><p>Canada</p></td><td><p>MuleSoft-managed control plane hosted in Canada. See <a href="../hyperforce/#deploy-mule-apps-to-control-planes">Deploy Mule Apps to Control Planes</a>.</p></td><td><p>Canadian data residency</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Japan Cloud</p></td><td><p><code>jp1.platform.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><p>Japan</p></td><td><p>MuleSoft-managed control plane hosted in Japan. See <a href="../hyperforce/#deploy-mule-apps-to-control-planes">Deploy Mule Apps to Control Planes</a>.</p></td><td><p>Japanese data residency</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>MuleSoft Government Cloud</p></td><td><p><code>gov.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><p>United States</p></td><td><p>Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP)-compliant control plane managed by MuleSoft, suitable for US government agencies.</p></td><td><p>FedRAMP authorized. See <a href="../gov-cloud/">MuleSoft Government Cloud Overview</a>. See also <a href="#mq-support">Anypoint MQ in Government Cloud</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Customer-Hosted (Anypoint Platform Private Cloud Edition (PCE))</p></td><td><p>N/A (self-hosted)</p></td><td><p>Customer-managed</p></td><td><p>Self-hosted control plane deployed in your own data center, virtual server, or third-party cloud (for example, Amazon Web Services (AWS)). You control where assets and metadata are stored.</p></td><td><p>Full data sovereignty. See <a href="../private-cloud/latest/">Anypoint Platform PCE</a>. Some Anypoint Platform components arenât supported.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Available Runtime Regions by Control Plane

This table lists the runtime regions available for each control plane:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Control Plane</th><th>Geographic Area</th><th>Region</th><th>Region Name</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td rowspan="12"><p><a href="https://anypoint.mulesoft.com">US Cloud</a></p></td><td rowspan="5"><p>North America</p></td><td><p>US East (N. Virginia)</p></td><td><p><code>us-east</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>US East (Ohio)</p></td><td><p><code>us-east-2</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>US West (N. California)</p></td><td><p><code>us-west</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>US West (Oregon)</p></td><td><p><code>us-west-2</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Canada (Montreal)</p></td><td><p><code>ca-central</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>South America</p></td><td><p>Brazil (SÃ£o Paulo)</p></td><td><p><code>br-south</code></p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><p>Asia Pacific</p></td><td><p>Singapore (Singapore)</p></td><td><p><code>ap-southeast</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Australia (Sydney)</p></td><td><p><code>ap-southeast-2</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Japan (Tokyo)</p></td><td><p><code>ap-northeast</code></p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><p>Europe</p></td><td><p>Ireland (Dublin)</p></td><td><p><code>eu-west</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Germany (Frankfurt)</p></td><td><p><code>eu-central</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>UK (London)</p></td><td><p><code>eu-west-2</code></p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p><a href="https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com">EU Cloud</a></p></td><td rowspan="2"><p>Europe</p></td><td><p>Ireland (Dublin)</p></td><td><p><code>eu-west</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Germany (Frankfurt)</p></td><td><p><code>eu-central</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://ca1.platform.mulesoft.com">Canada Cloud</a></p></td><td><p>North America</p></td><td><p>Canada (Montreal)</p></td><td><p><code>ca-central-1</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://jp1.platform.mulesoft.com">Japan Cloud</a></p></td><td><p>Asia Pacific</p></td><td><p>Japan (Tokyo)</p></td><td><p><code>ap-northeast-1</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://gov.anypoint.mulesoft.com">MuleSoft Government Cloud</a></p></td><td><p>US Government</p></td><td><p>US GovCloud (West)</p></td><td><p><code>us-gov-west</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Anypoint MQ in Government Cloud

You canât access or configure some Anypoint MQ features, such as client apps and unencrypted queues.
For information, see [Anypoint MQ in MuleSoft Government Cloud](../mq/#mq-in-govcloud).

## Runtime Plane Hosting Options

MuleSoft provides different options for hosting the runtime plane:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Runtime Option</th><th>Description</th><th>Managed By</th><th>Key Characteristics</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>CloudHub 2.0</p></td><td><p>Fully managed, containerized integration platform as a service (iPaaS) for deploying APIs and integrations as lightweight containers in the cloud. Supports deployments across 12 regions globally with single or shared tenancy.</p></td><td><p>MuleSoft</p></td><td><p>Auto-scaling, high availability, dynamic infrastructure. See <a href="../cloudhub-2/">CloudHub 2.0</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>CloudHub</p></td><td><p>MuleSoftâs cloud-based environment for hosting Mule runtime. Provides high availability, clustering, failover, and load balancing.</p></td><td><p>MuleSoft</p></td><td><p>See <a href="../cloudhub/">CloudHub</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Runtime Fabric</p></td><td><p>Container service for running Mule applications and API gateways in your own data center or third-party cloud. Bundled with Docker and Kubernetes for high availability, failover, clustering, and load balancing.</p></td><td><p>Customer (infrastructure) and MuleSoft (orchestration)</p></td><td><p>See <a href="../runtime-fabric/latest/">Anypoint Runtime Fabric</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Hybrid Standalone Runtimes</p></td><td><p>Mule runtime engine hosted in your environment (physical server, virtual machine (VM), or cloud like AWS/Azure) with centralized management through the MuleSoft control plane.</p></td><td><p>Customer (infrastructure) and MuleSoft (management)</p></td><td><p>Deployments within your trust boundary with cloud-based centralized management. See <a href="../hybrid-standalone/">Hybrid Standalone</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Standalone Runtimes</p></td><td><p>Mule runtime engine on a single instance with no required connection to the control plane. You manage high availability (HA), failover, clustering, and load balancing manually. Suited for air-gapped or disconnected environments.</p></td><td><p>Customer (fully self-managed)</p></td><td><p>See <a href="../mule-runtime/latest/mule-standalone">Run Mule Runtime Engine On-Premises</a>.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Component Support by Runtime Option

Not all Anypoint Platform components are available in each runtime option:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Component</th><th>CloudHub 2.0</th><th>CloudHub</th><th>Runtime Fabric</th><th>Standalone</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Mule runtime engine</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Anypoint MQ</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Anypoint Object Store v2</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>No <sup>1</sup></p></td><td><p>No <sup>1</sup></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Anypoint DataGraph</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connectors <sup>2</sup></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

1 Runtime Fabric and standalone runtimes support Mule clustering instead of Object Store v2. On
Runtime Fabric, multiple replicas of the same application can share objects. See
[Cluster Runtime Instances](../mule-runtime/latest/choosing-the-right-clustering-topology).  
2 File system connectors (for example, Anypoint Connector for File) are not supported on Runtime
Fabric because deployed apps lack file system access.

## Control Plane and Runtime Plane Compatibility

This matrix shows which runtime plane options are supported in each control plane:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Runtime Plane</th><th>US Cloud</th><th>EU Cloud</th><th>Canada Cloud</th><th>Japan Cloud</th><th>MuleSoft Government Cloud</th><th>Anypoint Platform PCE</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>CloudHub 2.0</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>CloudHub</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Runtime Fabric</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Hybrid Standalone Runtimes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Choosing a Hosting Option

Anypoint Platform lets you host the control plane and runtime plane independently. Depending on your
requirements, you can choose from these options:

- Use Anypoint Platform in the MuleSoft-managed control plane and runtime plane.
- Host and manage the runtime plane within your own infrastructure while connecting it with the
  MuleSoft-managed control plane.
- Host both the control plane and runtime plane locally within your infrastructure.

See the [Control Plane Hosting Options](#control-plane-hosting-options) and
[Runtime Plane Hosting Options](#runtime-plane-hosting-options) tables for available options, and
the [Control Plane and Runtime Plane Compatibility](#support-matrix-control-plane) to verify which
combinations are supported.
